steps.title: Find the linguistic signal
steps.content: The first sentence uses the phrase “broadly speaking” to introduce a general claim about auroras and the Sun. This signals that specific details or mechanisms will follow immediately.

steps.title: Match the transition to the signal
steps.content: “Specifically” directly continues the “broadly speaking” language by introducing the precise mechanism of how the Sun causes auroras. The other three introduce wrong relationships: “Similarly” suggests parallel information, “Nevertheless” signals contradiction, and “Hence” suggests a conclusion rather than explanation. This matches choice A — A is correct.
